ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-21-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sean Miller appeared by zoom for Carlos Ausejo.
Jose Marenco in propria persona.

Mr. Marenco stated that he was here to get paid for the work he did, that the attorney told him that there was no money to pay him, questioned why for the last two months someone has been working on the house if there is no money, stated that he provided some pictures to the attorney and that the attorney told him that he couldn't talk to him anymore because he can't see the work. Mr. Miller stated that the house was a severely deteriorated property, that he saw no evidence of any new copper pipes, that the creditor's claim expired, and that he could not see any evidence of any work being completed. Mr. Marenco stated that he did not know that Carlos passed away until three months later, that he was late due to COVID illness and living in a shelter, and that a year and a half later was when he was able to work on this. The court stated that the biggest problem is that the claim was late and that it will not order that he be paid from the estate. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Apr-10-2023 Continued To May-15-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Pat Miller, esq., via Zoom, for Carlos Ausejo, Administrator; Jose Marenco, in court, in propria persona, Creditor; Arturo Rodriguez, in court, in propria persona, interested Party.

The interpreter stated for the record: Her name is Marissa Ayerdi. Her Certification Number is 300927. She has an oath on file with the Court. The language to be interpreted is Spanish to English and English to Spanish. Madam Interpreter reports that after a discussion with Mr. Marenco, it is her understanding that he will offer testimony in English to the best of his ability and will stand-by to assist as needed.

At the court's query, Mr. Miller reports as to why he did not appear at the last calling, stating that it was due to an oversight for which he takes total responsibility. The court acknowledges Mr. Miller's report and the Order to Appear is taken off calendar. The court acknowledges Mr. Marenco's appearance this morning. Mr. Miller confirms that he has reviewed Mr. Marenco's creditors claim and supporting documents and reports that he has visited the property himself and reports of the dilapidated condition of the property and that he did not see any evidence of the work that Mr. Marenco is requesting to be reimbursed for. Mr. Miller reminds the court that no action was taken by the Creditor on the rejection of his claim within the time allowed. Mr. Marenco offers advocacy for his creditors claim and confirms that no permits were requested to perform the work at request of the homeowner. Mr. Rodriguez reports that he had lived on the property and refutes Mr. Marenco's report and confirms that a different plumber had provided plumbing services. The court acknowledges the testimony regarding Mr. Marenco's creditors claim and confirms with Mr. Marenco that he has submitted all proof regarding his reported plumbing work. The court confirms with Mr. Miller that the real property has been sold. Mr. Miller requests time to complete and file a petition accordingly. The court acknowledges Mr. Miller's request and reminds counsel that the court expects a petition to be filed prior to the next calling. The court also asks Mr. Miller to discuss the creditors claim issue with Mr. Marenco in hopes of resolving that matter.

The Status is continued to May-15-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods/Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Feb-27-2023 Continued To Apr-10-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jose A. Morenco (creditor) in propria persona.

Mr. Morenco stated that he did some plumbing work on the whole building, that he wants to get paid for his work and for the materials, that it's about $14,000.00 and described the work he performed (re-coppered the whole building, replaced water lines). Mr. Morenco also stated that he did some emergency work for $9,000.00, that he told the estate that they owed him money and they said no, that he did some carpentry and some small repairs, but he is not asking for payment of those services, and that he is here to press his claim. PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-15-2020 Continued To Sep-29-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Omar Krashna appeared by telephone for Arturo Rodriguez (Petitioner).
Pat Miller appeared by telephone for Jesus Sanchez (Petioner).

The Court stated that it received the joint case management statement ("CMS"), that it was very helpful and that the issue is whether the will that Mr. Rodriguez wants to be admitted is admissible. The Court also questions why this matter is being held up by the civil matter, stated that the civil action is for quiet title and has nothing to do with the will, that this matter has been here for a year and a half, and that it needs to be resolved. Mr. Krashna stated that matter needs to be advanced because the estate property is being lost in foreclosure. Mr. Miller stated that the problem lies in the issue of the arrearages, that he filed a CMS and questions whether the Court has seen it. The Court informed Mr. Miller that it will not read the individual CMS, and stated that it ordered a joint CMS and that the joint CMS is all it is going to read. Mr. Miller stated that the Court can set a trial, or an evidentiary hearing, but it will not change the circumstances, that no one has the money to pay the mortgage, and that they need to have a Special Administrator appointed to sell the property otherwise, the property will be lost. The Court noted that a general Administrator can sell the property and suggests Mr. Miller's Client because he is the Administrator in the other case. Mr. Krashna stated that he would object and that his Client wants the will to be admitted. The Court noted that the joint CMS shows that Mr. Miller has seven witnesses, but indicates testimony unknown, questions how those witnesses can be binding witnesses and stated that this does not comply with the Court's order and that it does not help for trial setting purposes. Mr. Miller stated that a lot of those witnesses haven't been deposed, that they have spoken with some of them, but some of them have Attorneys and that he has not spoken with them. The Court stated that this is not a civil action, that probate does not go on for years and years, that the parties need to figure out how to work this out, and that it is inclined to continue this for two weeks to give Mr. Miller a chance to comply with the Court's order, or it will impose sanctions. The Court also noted that the CMS shows that Mr. Miller does not have enough evidence to support his will contest, stated that the parties are to meet and confer starting today to provide a meaningful joint CMS that complies with the Court's order and that the joint CMS must provide a binding witness list. In addition, The Court stated that it will set a hearing within a few months and informed the parties that it will not hesitate imposing sanctions on either side. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, the Petition for Letters of Administration, and the case management hearing are continued to September 29, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint case management statement is to be filed by September 22, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
